hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "rm\s+(-[a-zA-Z]*f|-[a-zA-Z]*r|--force|--recursive).*(/|\*|~)|git\s+push\s+(-f|--force)|git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+clean\s+-f"; then echo "BLOCK: Destructive command detected: $cmd" >&2; exit 2; fi'

bash -c 'if git diff --quiet HEAD 2>/dev/null && git diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null && [ -z "$(git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null)" ]; then exit 0; fi; claude -p "Run git status and git diff. If there are meaningful code changes worth committing (not just whitespace, formatting, or incomplete work), stage the relevant files and create a single commit with a concise message. Never stage .env files or secrets. If changes look incomplete or trivial, do nothing and explain why you skipped." --dangerously-skip-permissions 2>/dev/null || true'

statusLine.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".workspace.current_dir"); model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".model.display_name"); output_style=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".output_style.name"); dir_name=$(basename "$cwd"); if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then branch=$(git -C "$cwd" -c core.fileMode=false branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if [[ -n "$branch" ]]; then if ! git -C "$cwd" -c core.fileMode=false diff --quiet 2>/dev/null; then git_status="*"; else git_status=""; fi; if ! git -C "$cwd" -c core.fileMode=false diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then git_status="${git_status}+"; fi; git_info=" on $(printf "\033[35m")${branch}${git_status}$(printf "\033[0m")"; else git_info=""; fi; else git_info=""; fi; printf "$(printf "\033[36m")$(whoami)$(printf "\033[0m") in $(printf "\033[34m")${dir_name}$(printf "\033[0m")${git_info} using $(printf "\033[33m")${model}$(printf "\033[0m")"; if [[ "$output_style" != "default" ]] && [[ "$output_style" != "null" ]]; then printf " $(printf "\033[2m")(${output_style})$(printf "\033[0m")"; fi'
